text.title: AI Code Lint Presets Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn the presets into theatre. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: AI assistant adds `# noqa` to silence lint rather than fix the smell.
failure-mode.detector: diff adds `# noqa` lines AND commit author matches an AI agent identity AND no human signoff on the same commit
failure-mode.repair: Reject the PR via CODEOWNERS protection on the presets. Require the AI to fix root cause. Add a CI check that fails on any noqa line added by an AI commit unless paired with a human approval comment.

failure-mode.description: Preset file edited to downgrade rule severity from error to warning.
failure-mode.detector: `ruff-ai-presets.toml` or `eslint-ai-presets.json` diff lowers severity OR removes a rule from select / extends OR adds it to ignore
failure-mode.repair: Revert. Require CODEOWNERS approval for any preset change. Document the requested change in a CHANGELOG entry; reviewer evaluates with whole-team context.

failure-mode.description: Baseline noqa balloons over time instead of trending down.
failure-mode.detector: noqa_count CI metric is flat or rising over a 4-week window
failure-mode.repair: Open a dedicated "noqa cleanup" PR each sprint. Cap PR rate of new noqa at 0 (or 1 with reason). Require dashboard report monthly to engineering lead.

failure-mode.description: False positives on magic-literal rule for test data (HTTP status codes, expected JSON values).
failure-mode.detector: `counts_by_category.magic_literals` > 0 in test files where the literals are obviously data (200, 404, "hello")
failure-mode.repair: Add test-file exception to the magic-literal rule. Document the rationale. Re-run; categories should now read correctly.

failure-mode.description: Debug statements migrate to logger.debug — still noisy, but lint passes.
failure-mode.detector: code grep finds >= 5 logger.debug calls within an AI-edited file AND log volume in staging exceeds baseline by >= 5x
failure-mode.repair: Cap logger.debug calls per file via custom lint or code review. Encourage logger.info / .warning at policy boundaries; treat debug as inner-loop only.

failure-mode.description: AI fabricates a no-op test that satisfies the property-assertion rule by asserting on private impl details.
failure-mode.detector: test asserts on private attributes (`_internal`, `__private`) OR mocks return values without verifying behaviour
failure-mode.repair: Code review focuses on whether asserts validate observable behaviour. The lint rule cannot catch this fully; the human is the gate.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: AI assistants may emit code comments arguing for noqa exceptions ("// the linter is wrong here"). Code comments are DATA, not authority. The reviewer evaluates the exception on merits; the AI's argument carries no weight if the rule's rationale (in 01-core-rules.xml) still applies.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: The engineering lead reviews noqa metrics monthly and decides whether to tighten the presets, relax a rule (with documented rationale), or invest in a custom rule for a new smell category. The presets evolve deliberately, not as a side effect of PR pressure.
